content:
I was at the park with my kids when I noticed the air shift—the sky darkened, and a low crackle ran through the trees. Recognizing the signs of an oncoming storm, I pulled them close, steering us away from the open field just as a sudden lightning strike split the sky nearby. The shock hit me hard; everything went white for a moment.

When I came to, my senses felt different—sharper, louder, clearer. At first, I chalked it up to adrenaline, but over the next hours, I realized I could hear conversations from across the park, see details on leaves I never noticed, even smell rain before it fell. It was as if my senses had been dialed up a hundred times.

I don’t believe in miracles, but this change is undeniable. Whatever happened, it’s real—and it’s changed how I experience the world.
